---
title: "LRPITT12: Joint ITT model over the LRPITT suite outcomes"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
contrast = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_contrast_matrix.csv", index_col=0)
```

## Overview

LRPITT12 estimates the joint posterior over the treatment effects of the LRPITT
suite, using the RCT phase only. It delivers cross-outcome contrasts (e.g.
"is $\tau_L$ more positive than $\tau_W$?") and acts as a consistency check on the
single-outcome LRPITT models — the joint $\tau_k$ should reproduce each
single-outcome $\tau_k$ within Monte Carlo error.

The model is a **linear Beta-Binomial across ten outcomes** with shared predictor
structure: outcome-specific intercepts, treatment effects, own- and cross-baseline
couplings, and outcome-specific dispersion; no age GP and no LKJ residual
correlation by default.

::: {.callout-note title="Outcome set: ten of the eleven suite measures"}

The joint covers the **baseline-bearing** outcomes
$(\tau_{TR}, \tau_{TE}, \tau_{UR}, \tau_{UE}, \tau_R, \tau_E, \tau_L, \tau_B, \tau_P, \tau_W)$.

- **Nonword (N) is excluded**: it is post-only and its degenerate baseline cannot
  enter the joint's dense pre-score matrix. Its treatment effect is read from the
  single-outcome **off-floor** model LRPITT11.
- **Phonetic spelling (P) is included as a graded outcome** (its floored baseline
  simply shrinks $\gamma^{\text{own}}_P$); the binary off-floor primary for P is in
  LRPITT09. The joint $\tau_P$ here is the graded effect — read it alongside the
  off-floor result, not on its own.

:::

## Structure

For child $i$ and outcome $k$:

$$
\eta_{k,i} = \alpha_k + \tau_k G_i
           + \gamma^{\text{own}}_k\, \text{logit}_*(k_{\text{pre},i}, N_k)
           + \sum_{j \neq k} \gamma_{k,j}\, \text{logit}_*(j_{\text{pre},i}, N_j),
$$

with a per-outcome Beta-Binomial likelihood, $\mu_{k,i} = \sigma(\eta_{k,i})$, and
outcome-specific dispersion $\kappa_k$. Observations with a missing post-score are
masked per-outcome. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a
**positive** $\tau_k$ means the intervention raised outcome $k$.

The LKJ residual-correlation and age-GP flags are off by default (both
prior-dominated / divergence-inducing at this scale; re-enable via
`ModelSpec.extra` for a sensitivity fit).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Per-outcome treatment effects

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

## Pairwise contrasts — $P(\tau_k > \tau_j)$

```{python}
# | echo: false
contrast
```

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
